business.headline: Why alcohol sales are set to slow globally over next 10 years but grow in IndiaAs global alcohol consumption enters a phase of slowdown, India appears to be charting a very different growth path. What lies behind this contrasting trend?

SpaceX IPO could create 4,400 employee millionaires. Here's howSpaceX's planned IPO at $135 a share could make more than 4,400 current and former employees millionaires. The public listing could also widen retail participation and trigger fresh spending by long-time equity holders.

Markets end lower as IT slump and US inflation fears weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex fell 150.63 points, or 0.20%, to close at 73,832.55, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 53.35 points, or 0.23%, to settle at 23,161.60.

India overtakes the world's biggest economy in construction growthThe country has been investing heavily in highways, railways, airports, ports, metro systems and logistics networks. These projects are not only creating jobs and boosting economic activity today but are also laying the foundation for future growth.

Is your CIBIL score below 730? RBI's new rules may make loans harder to getThe RBI's Expected Credit Loss rules from April 1, 2027 could make banks stricter with borrowers below a 730 CIBIL score. The shift may raise rates, collateral demands and rejection risks for applicants with thin or irregular credit histories.

8th Pay Commission: Will state government employees get salary hikes too?The 8th Central Pay Commission has been constituted by the Union government to review salaries, pensions, allowances and other service conditions of Central government employees and pensioners.

From PAN, TDS to tax regime: 7 Form 16 checks you can't miss before filing ITRFor many salaried employees, Form 16 is the starting point of ITR filing. But before you file your return, have you checked whether your PAN, TDS credits and tax regime details are actually correct?

Gold and silver are falling from their peaks. 3 things investors must do nowThe shine appears to be fading, at least for now. After soaring to all-time highs earlier this year, gold and silver prices have come under pressure, leaving investors asking a familiar question: is it time to buy more, hold on, or head for the exit?

No E30 at pumps yet, so why has India exempted higher ethanol petrol from tax?India has exempted E22, E25, E27 and E30 petrol blends from excise duty despite no retail rollout yet. The move sets up a policy framework for higher ethanol fuels as questions persist over vehicle compatibility and costs.

Infosys, HCL Tech, TCS fall up to 3%: Why are IT stocks falling today?The Nifty IT index fell 2.67% to 27,525.80, emerging as the worst-performing sector on Dalal Street as of 9:50 am. The decline also weighed heavily on the Sensex, where IT majors were among the biggest losers.

Sensex, Nifty fall amid West Asia tensions; IT stocks tumble over 2%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 263.34 points, or 0.36%, to 73,719.84 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 70.70 points, or 0.30%, to 23,144.25 as of 9:24 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early indicators pointed to a weak start for domestic equities. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,069 at around 7:43 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open below Wednesday's closing level of 23,214.95.

OpenDoor lays off entire India team of 250 in AI-driven overhaulOpenDoor is winding down its India operations and moving nearly 250 roles to the United States. The shift to smaller AI-native teams has sparked fresh questions about AI's impact on offshoring.

India scraps excise duty on petrol with 22% to 30% ethanol blendIndia has exempted petrol containing 22% to 30% ethanol from excise duty. The move is aimed at encouraging higher ethanol use and reducing reliance on imported crude oil.

Who borrows the most in India today: Gen Z, millennials or older Indians?India's love affair with credit is growing. As digital lending apps and easy EMIs make borrowing more accessible than ever, one question stands out: who is borrowing the most? Is it young Gen Z, middle-aged millennials, or older Indians?

Sebi proposes new salary disclosure rules for asset managersIn a consultation paper released on Wednesday, Sebi suggested that AMCs should be allowed to disclose the combined remuneration paid to executive-level employees along with the total number of such employees.

Markets end marginally higher as FMCG, banks gains cushion profit bookingThe BSE Sensex rose 64.42 points, or 0.09%, to close at 73,983.18. However, the NSE Nifty50 slipped 27.15 points, or 0.12%, to settle at 23,214.95.

EU widens Russia sanctions net, India-based entities among 50 firms targetedThe European Commission has proposed a new Russia sanctions package that could restrict India-based companies. The move widens scrutiny of third-country trade networks, but it still needs unanimous EU approval.

Zepto moves closer to IPO launch: What investors should watchAccording to the updated filing, Zepto's IPO will consist of a fresh issue of shares worth Rs 8,010 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of 113 million shares by existing investors.

No 8th Pay Commission yet, but a DA hike could be around the cornerFresh inflation data has raised the possibility of a Dearness Allowance (DA) increase from July 2026 for central government employees and pensioners. The likely move could offer interim relief while the 8th Pay Commission continues to examine wider salary and pension demands.

entertainment.headline: Women's panel summons Pranit More, Himanshu Jangra over Rs 370 biryani row The National Commission for Women has summoned comedian Pranit More and Himanshu Jangra over remarks made during a Gurugram stand-up show that allegedly glorified sexual coercion.

4 new movies and shows releasing this week on Prime Video, ZEE5 From Suriya's blockbuster action drama Karuppu to the suspense-filled crime thriller Raakh, this week's OTT slate offers something for every viewer. Reality show Maa Hai Na and Bengali mystery drama Taarkata round out the list of fresh releases arriving on Prime Video and ZEE5.

Kangana Ranaut clarifies 'very British' nurse uniform remark: Reforms were made Kangana Ranaut clarified that her comments on nurses' uniforms reflected a personal view and acknowledged that reforms had already been implemented years ago. The actor spoke about the issue while promoting Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata, in which she plays a nurse.

Spider-Man: Brand New Day gets early release in India Peter Parker's next big-screen outing is inching closer, and Indian fans are getting a small head start. Sony Pictures has rolled out a fresh update for Spider-Man: Brand New Day as anticipation around the film continues to mount.

My queen Madhuri Dixit is a scene-stealer: Priyanka Chopra reviews Maa Behen Priyanka Chopra Jonas praised Suresh Triveni's Maa Behen for its clever storytelling and powerful performances, especially highlighting Madhuri Dixit's captivating presence.

5 new movies and shows releasing this week on Netflix, JioHotstar Netflix and JioHotstar are rolling out five titles across genres this week. The line-up ranges from horror-comedy and crime thrillers to true crime and international drama.

Was Madhuri Dixit's blouse a fight against patriarchy? Maa Behen director answers Suresh Triveni addressed the debate over the sleeveless blouse worn by Madhuri Dixit's character in Maa Behen. He said the garment reflects personal choice within the story, even as viewers read it as a symbol of patriarchy.

Jana Nayagan leak case: Absconding mastermind arrested in Chennai The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested the alleged mastermind in the Jana Nayagan footage leak case. The latest arrests have taken the total to 19 as the probe and piracy warning continue.

Rs 1.7 crore for Welcome To The Jungle? Akshay Kumar reacts to fee rumours At the trailer launch of Welcome to the Jungle, Akshay Kumar dismissed reports that he charged Rs 1.7 crore for the film. He said the project matters to him because Neeraj Vora wrote the story.

Hrithik Roshan replaces SRK in Jailer 2, reunites with Rajinikanth after 40 years Hrithik Roshan has joined Rajinikanth's Jailer 2 for a special cameo, with his scenes set to be shot in Chennai later this month. The appearance reunites them on screen 40 years after Bhagwaan Dada and adds to the sequel's star-heavy line-up.

Main Vaapas Aaunga review: Diljit Dosanjh leads Imtiaz Ali's weakest filmRelease Date:Thu Jun 11, 2026 Main Vaapas Aaunga movie review: Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga follows a grandson decoding his dying grandfather's Partition memories. The film reaches for grief and belonging, but its pacing and politics blunt the impact.

Ranvir Shorey defends comedy, criticises social media outrage amid Pranit More row Ranvir Shorey has indirectly backed comedian Pranit More amid backlash over a viral live show clip, calling out "online outrage" against comedy.

Lagaan cast then and now: Where are they 25 years later? As Lagaan turns 25, the film is set for a theatrical re-release and a reunion of the people who made it unforgettable. On that note, here's a look at what the villagers of Champaner are doing in 2026.

CM Vijay's Mersal to re-release in theatres as actor-politician's pre-birthday gift Director Atlee's Mersal will return to theatres in Chennai and Chengalpattu on June 19 ahead of Vijay's birthday. The re-release gives fans a big-screen celebration as Jana Nayagan's release remains unclear.

Main Vaapas Aaunga, Governor, Backrooms: The story of great summer box office clash Indian cinemas are set for a crowded June 12 with nine releases across genres and languages. The line-up is being watched as a key box office test for the summer theatrical business.

PM Modi mourns Bharathiraja, calls him towering figure of Tamil cinema in tribute Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid tribute to iconic Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja a day after the veteran filmmaker died at 84. His message highlighted the director's lasting impact on Tamil cinema and his acclaimed portrayal of rural life.

Welcome to The Jungle trailer: Akshay in 'fake' jungle madness and wildest cast ever The trailer for Welcome to the Jungle is out. Here's everything you need to know about Akshay Kumar's comedy adventure, including its cast, story hints, and what the trailer reveals.

7 savage jokes Pranit More could've cracked instead of rewarding Rs 370 biryani guy Pranit More rewarded the viral Gurugram man who said he expected sex after buying a woman Rs 370 worth of biryani. The exchange drew backlash, largely because many felt the comedian could have read the room better and turned the joke on the man himself.

Netflix not looking at Kannada cinema now: Kannada actor Sanchith Sanjeev Kannada actor Sanchith Sanjeev, nephew of Kichcha Sudeep, spoke to India Today about his debut film Mango Pachcha, challenges in Kannada cinema, nepotism and filmmaking dreams. He also made some explosive claims about the OTT space in Kannada cinema.

general news.headline: Blockade impartial: US rains Hellfire missiles on Jalveer, 20 Indians rescuedAmerican forces on Thursday hit oil tanker M/T Jalveer, which was being crewed by 20 Indian sailors, with two Hellfire missiles. All 20 have been rescued. This was the third attack in four days on a ship being crewed by Indians in the Gulf of Oman. The US said it was enforcing the blockade on Iran impartially.

Trump warns of Iran strikes tonight, Venezuela-like takeover of its oil in futureIn a post on Truth Social, Trump claims that Iran's military capabilities have already been severely weakened and says a major US military strike is imminent.

Pakistan launches 6 satellites to watch India, Isro's NavIC still in crisisPakistan has launched six Chinese-backed Earth-observation satellites as India struggles to restore NavIC. The contrast has sharpened concern over surveillance capability and the reliability of India's sovereign navigation network.

Attacks must end: India condemns US strike on vessel near Oman that killed 3 IndiansThe government said it had summoned the US Chargé d'affaires on Wednesday to formally protest a strike on a ship off the coast of Oman that killed 3 Indian nationals. Three ships carrying Indian crew members have been hit by the US Navy in the last four days, with one struck by US Hellfire missiles on Thursday.

10 rounds in 5 seconds: Bishnoi gang shoots gym owner dead, warns of more attacksA gym operator was shot dead by motorcycle-borne attackers near Fawwara Chowk in Hansi during an early morning exercise session. A young woman was injured by bullet fragments as police and forensic teams began an investigation after CCTV footage surfaced.

IND A vs AFG A Highlights: Rain, Afghanistan ruin India's party in DambullaIndia A (IND) vs Afghanistan A (AFG) Highlights, Tri-Series 2026: Afghanistan A (177/2 in 25.5 overs), chasing 294 in 38 overs. And that's it from Dambulla. India A have lost by 4 runs via DLS method. Captain Imran Mir and senior batter Bahir Shah added 108 runs in just 99 balls. They put India under immense pressure.

Monsoon has stalled: What's blocking its vigorous march across India?After racing across large parts of southern and northeastern India, the southwest monsoon has entered a slower phase, raising questions about what comes next.

11 kids among 13 killed in airstrike in Afghanistan. UN doesn't name culprit PakPakistani airstrikes in eastern Afghanistan killed 13 civilians, including 11 children, said the Taliban. The UN, which said it documented the casualties, faced criticism after its statement as it did not name the country behind the strikes.

Another Dravid in India colours: BCCI picks Rahul's son Anvay in U19 squadRahul Dravid's younger son, Anvay, has been named in India's Under-19 one-day squad for Sri Lanka. The call-up rewards his domestic form and begins his pathway in the next World Cup cycle.

I gave up my H-1B visa: Ex-Microsoft techie explains why he returned to IndiaFrom winning the H-1B lottery on his first attempt to working at Microsoft, Hemant Shetty lived the life countless Indian professionals dream of. However, Shetty returned to India. The restrictions on entrepreneurship and uncertainties linked to the H-1B visa norms made him choose India over the US.

UP woman raped by father-in-law at gunpoint, husband gives triple talaq over phoneA woman in UP's Bahraich has accused her father-in-law of repeatedly raping her at gunpoint and alleged her husband later pronounced triple talaq over the phone. Police have filed a case against five members of the woman's in-laws and made no arrests so far.

Thunderstorms, hailstorms and heavy rain across large parts of India on FridayThe IMD has issued widespread weather warnings for Friday as the monsoon advances and multiple systems intensify conditions. The alerts signal severe rain, thunderstorms, hail and parallel heatwave conditions across several regions.

The Great Millennial Career Crisis: I did everything right, but still felt stuckMillennials were told that education, hard work, and career loyalty would lead to success and stability. Yet many now face burnout, financial stress, career stagnation, and fears about AI. Through personal experiences and broader workplace trends, let's see why the traditional formula for success no longer feels certain for many.

Baseless rumours: Congress rejects TMC merger buzz amid crisis in West BengalThe speculation comes amid a split within the TMC's legislative and parliamentary wings, barely a month after the party was voted out of power in West Bengal, securing only 80 seats in the 294-member Assembly.

No intention of changing sides, stand firmly with Mamata ji: Shatrughan SinhaShatrughan Sinha dismissed speculation of joining the TMC rebel camp, declaring his unwavering support for Mamata Banerjee and saying he would stand by her during the party's crisis just as she had supported him during difficult phases.

Form human shield: Islamist Jamaat to march to India border over 'push-ins'Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami and allied Islamist parties have announced that they would march to various border points with India on Friday protesting against the alleged Indian push-ins and killings near the frontier. The anti-Hasina student's NCP has called for Bangladeshis to form a human shield along the border even as high-level talks between India and Bangladesh are being held in New Delhi.

Godzilla El Nino to eat up Gujarat, Goa's monsoon rain, worsen water crisisClimate projections are drawing attention to potential shifts in India's rainy season, with implications that could extend far beyond weather forecasts.

Main Vaapas Aaunga review: Diljit Dosanjh leads Imtiaz Ali's weakest filmRelease Date:Thu Jun 11, 2026Main Vaapas Aaunga movie review: Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga follows a grandson decoding his dying grandfather's Partition memories. The film reaches for grief and belonging, but its pacing and politics blunt the impact.

Delhi's Aslam Rickshawala impresses tourists with Hindi, Spanish and English. WatchA video of the interaction began with Delhi's Aslam Rickshawala introducing himself to the camera before greeting the visitors in Spanish.

Live: Not right to abandon sinking ship, says Babul Supriyo as he sides with MamataTMC Crisis Live Updates: As the political turmoil within the Trinamool Congress deepened, senior MP Shatrughan Sinha rejected reports linking his name to an alleged list of rebel MPs, asserting that he remains firmly loyal to party chief Mamata Banerjee. The Congress, meanwhile, rejected reports of a merger with TMC, saying they were baseless. Mamata Banerjee returned to West Bengal after concluding her three-day visit to Delhi, during which she met INDIA bloc leaders, including senior Congress leader Sonia Gandhi. Abhishek Banerjee remains in Delhi, with no clarity yet on when he will return to Kolkata, even as the West Bengal CID has summoned him in connection with the forged-signatures row. Stay tuned to India Today for the latest updates.

7 savage jokes Pranit More could've cracked instead of rewarding Rs 370 biryani guyPranit More rewarded the viral Gurugram man who said he expected sex after buying a woman Rs 370 worth of biryani. The exchange drew backlash, largely because many felt the comedian could have read the room better and turned the joke on the man himself.

Bodies were unrecognisable: Two officials recall horror of AI171 Ahmedabad crashThe investigation into the crash—one of India's worst aviation disasters—remains ongoing. A year later, two key officials involved in the response, Dr Rakesh Joshi and then Ahmedabad Police Commissioner GS Malik (now Gujarat DGP), have reflected on the coordinated multi-agency effort during the large-scale relief and recovery operations.

Priced at Rs 7.99 lakh, this 5-star SUV crossed 5 lakh sales milestonePriced from Rs 7.99 lakh (ex-showroom), the Hyundai Venue has crossed the 8 lakh domestic sales milestone in India, becoming one of the country's most successful compact SUVs since its 2019 debut.

No subscription needed: Here's how you can watch World Cup for free in IndiaThe FIFA World Cup 2026 is all set to begin, with the fans in India eagerly waiting to watch each game. Here's how you can watch the matches for free in India.

sports.headline: Barred from World Cup, Somalian referee to officiate major European fixture Omar Abdulkadir Artan has been appointed to referee the UEFA Super Cup in Salzburg on August 12. The call comes weeks after the Somalian official missed the World Cup despite holding a valid US visa.

Manu Bhaker to spearhead India's shooting challenge at Asian Games The National Rifle Association of India announced a 30-member shooting contingent for the 2026 Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya. The squad consists of as many as four double starters, including Manu Bhaker, who will compete in multiple events.

No rush on Stokes' future as England consider alcohol ban after nightclub row Rob Key is considering tougher restrictions, including a possible alcohol ban, after Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson were dropped following a London nightclub incident. Key offered no guarantees when it comes to Ben Stokes continuing as Test captain as the investigation continues.

After lucky escape vs Sri Lanka, India A lose to Afghanistan in Dambulla tri-series Talent tri-series, IND A vs AFG A: Afghanistan A mounted a fearless chase after rain revised India A's 349 into a 38-over target in Dambulla. Imran Mir and Bahir Shah stayed ahead of the DLS par before another downpour sealed the result on Thursday.

Men's ODI World Cup 2027 set to run from October 4 to November 21 The ICC has broadly approved an October 4 to November 21 window for the 2027 Men's ODI World Cup across South Africa, Zimbabwe and Namibia. The event restores a 14-team format, returns the men's World Cup to Africa, and sits alongside wider decisions on the next FTP and WTC structure.

Grand Slam pay war: Champions to earn Rs 46 crore as Wimbledon unveils record purse Wimbledon has raised its 2026 prize purse to a record £64.2 million, with singles champions set to earn £3.6 million each. The increase follows player protests over Grand Slam revenue sharing and lifts payouts across the draw.

Sooryavanshi trades aggression for restraint in 44-run cameo vs Afghanistan A Vaibhav Sooryavanshi showcased a different facet of his batting during India A's clash against Afghanistan A, scoring a brisk 44 off 22 balls while relying more on timing and placement than outright power. The 15-year-old struck nine boundaries and gave India A a flying start.

Another Dravid in India colours: BCCI picks Rahul's son Anvay in U19 squad Rahul Dravid's younger son, Anvay, has been named in India's Under-19 one-day squad for Sri Lanka. The call-up rewards his domestic form and begins his pathway in the next World Cup cycle.

All you need to know about FIFA World Cup opening ceremony The FIFA World Cup opens with three ceremonies across Mexico City, Toronto and Los Angeles over two days. Shakira will headline the first opening ceremony in Mexico on Thursday, performing her official tournament single "Dai Dai" live for the first time.

ICC annual rankings update: India remain the world's top ODI side India retained the No. 1 spot in the latest ICC men's ODI team rankings following the annual update, although their advantage at the top was reduced. India now lead second-placed New Zealand by five rating points, while Australia remain third.

Have you seen it? Kimi Antonelli trolls Kim Kardashian over his stolen towel Formula One: Kimi Antonelli's Monaco Grand Prix win shared the spotlight with a viral clip of Kim Kardashian using a towel laid out for him. The moment drew led to banter with the Mercedes driver joining in.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group K preview: Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal in a pickleGroup K offers a fascinating mix of established contenders and ambitious challengers. While Portugal and Colombia are expected to battle for top spot, DR Congo's resilience and Uzbekistan's defensive organisation could ensure every match carries significant weight in the race for qualification.

PV Sindhu's husband becomes an accidental coach. She has plenty to Sai about itFeeling slightly off, PV Sindhu asked husband Venkata Datta Sai to sit in her corner during her second-round match against Isharani Baruah in Sydney. She then beat her compatriot to reach the Australian Open quarter-finals on Thursday.

Australia add India-born Nikhil Chaudhary to T20Is squad for Bangladesh seriesDelhi-born all-rounder Nikhil Chaudhary could become the first Indian-born man to represent Australia in international cricket in more than 60 years after earning a call-up to the country's T20I squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

IND A vs AFG A Highlights: Rain, Afghanistan ruin India's party in DambullaIndia A (IND) vs Afghanistan A (AFG) Highlights, Tri-Series 2026: Afghanistan A (177/2 in 25.5 overs), chasing 294 in 38 overs. And that's it from Dambulla. India A have lost by 4 runs via DLS method. Captain Imran Mir and senior batter Bahir Shah added 108 runs in just 99 balls. They put India under immense pressure.

Harmanpreet Kaur vows fearless cricket as India prepare for Pakistan clashIndia captain Harmanpreet Kaur has vowed to play fearless cricket as her side gears up for a blockbuster start to its ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan at Edgbaston in Birmingham on Sunday.

LA 2028 working to avoid repeat of World Cup visa issues in US: IOC chiefLos Angeles Olympics organisers are working closely with U.S. authorities to ensure smooth entry procedures and avoid the visa complications that affected several stakeholders at the FIFA World Cup, IOC president Kirsty Coventry said on Wednesday.

FIFA World Cup arrives broken. Can football fix it again?The wait is over. Football's grandest stage is set once again. Yet before the spotlight turns to Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and a new generation eager to make its mark, the 2026 FIFA World Cup arrives under a cloud of scrutiny, carrying more off-field questions than any tournament would want on the eve of kick-off.

Chill, relax: FIFA chief shrugs off World Cup visa row in sharp exchange with reporterFIFA president Gianni Infantino said the organisation cannot dictate who the US government allows into the country for the World Cup, urging people to "chill and relax" over visa concerns.

FIFA World Cup 2026 by the numbers: Breaking down the largest edition everThe 2026 FIFA World Cup will open a 48-team tournament across the United States, Canada and Mexico on June 11. Its scale is unmatched, with 104 matches, 1,248 players and several records under threat.

No subscription needed: Here's how you can watch World Cup for free in IndiaThe FIFA World Cup 2026 is all set to begin, with the fans in India eagerly waiting to watch each game. Here's how you can watch the matches for free in India.

Ben Stokes made a mistake, but shouldn't be sacked as Test captain: Michael VaughanMichael Vaughan has defended Ben Stokes amid scrutiny over his curfew breach, arguing that while the England captain made a mistake, the incident does not justify removing him from the Test captaincy.

Making sure right people come to US: Trump on FIFA World Cup visa rowUS President Donald Trump said his administration is working to ensure that "the right people" are allowed into the country for the FIFA World Cup 2026 as the United States prepares to host the tournament.

Hitman in range: Rohit Sharma gets into groove for India vs Afghanistan ODIsRohit Sharma returned to India's white-ball nets and gradually found his range after a lay-off. The session eased immediate worries and showed a senior batter measuring himself against his own standards.

Explosive Richa Ghosh finds form, but India lose final T20 World Cup warm-upWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Richa Ghosh hit 68 off 36, but India lost to England in Cardiff in their final warm-up match. Her counterattack eased concerns before Sunday's World Cup opener, though India's top order still remains under scrutiny.

FIFA World Cup coverage in India headlined by Igor Stimac, Gurpreet SandhuWith the FIFA World Cup 2026 just days away, ZEE5 has assembled a star-studded multilingual commentary team featuring Bhaichung Bhutia, Gurpreet Singh Sandhu, Igor Stimac and I.M. Vijayan to bring the tournament closer to football fans across India.

After missing IPL, MS Dhoni promotes inaugural edition of Jharkhand T20 leagueMS Dhoni attended the opening ceremony of the inaugural Jharkhand T20 League in Ranchi on Wednesday. His presence gave the new tournament a high-profile start.

FIFA World Cup fever grips Kerala as God's own country prepares for tournamentWorld Cup fever has engulfed Kerala, with flags, cutouts and flex battles stretching across the state. Beneath the carnival sits a familiar truth: deep football culture, thin grassroots support and one more emotional wait for Messi, Ronaldo and Neymar.

Ben Stokes, Gus Atkinson dropped from England Test squad after nightclub incidentEngland captain Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have been dropped for the second Test against New Zealand after breaching a midnight curfew at a London nightclub. Stokes is reportedly considering his future as the Test captain of England.

Security breach sees Messi, Argentina's World Cup squad's passport details leakedAn official team sheet at Alabama's Jordan-Hare Stadium reportedly exposed the passport details of Lionel Messi and Argentina's entire squad before the Iceland friendly. The leak raised fresh concerns over player privacy and data security days before Argentina begin their World Cup defence.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
